name: Carnitine L Tartrate
summary: Human RCT evidence supports L-carnitine L-tartrate for exercise recovery and muscle-damage markers in trained or active adults; fat-loss claims are not established.
description: Human RCT evidence supports L-carnitine L-tartrate for exercise recovery and muscle-damage markers in trained or active adults; fat-loss claims are not established.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v8.2: L-carnitine L-tartrate has human exercise recovery evidence, but claims should stay recovery-specific.
claimMap.notes: v8.2: L-carnitine L-tartrate has human exercise recovery evidence, but claims should stay recovery-specific.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 1–2 g/day L-carnitine L-tartrate in exercise recovery studies

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 12930169. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 12930169. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: May reduce thyroid hormone effectiveness or worsen hypothyroidism symptoms in those on levothyroxine or other thyroid medication
increases seizure risk in people with a prior seizure history or seizure disorder and should be avoided in that group
supplementation raises trimethylamine N-oxide (TMAO) levels via gut bacterial metabolism which some cardiovascular research links to elevated cardiovascular risk
consult a clinician before use if on anticoagulant therapy.
